intent: Make the morning wake-alarm floor (lockdown-entry + MIN_WAKE_AFTER_LOCKDOWN_HOURS) correct for ANY shutdown time, not just the shutdown hours the static 05:00/05:15/05:30/06:00/07:00 unlock triggers happen to cover.
scope: Non-goal: did not remove or resize the existing staggered unlock triggers, which still handle console/GUI restore and remain a redundant fallback for the alarm in the common case
changes: Identified a gap in the earlier same-day fix: shutdown time is dynamically configurable (/etc/shutdown-schedule.conf, changed by the screen_locker sick-day feature) and can, in principle, be any hour. If a lockdown started late enough that entry+8h fell after the last static trigger (07:00), the alarm would silently never fire that morning -- confirmed via isolated arithmetic test (23:30 lockdown -> 07:30 floor, past every static trigger).

Enter script now schedules a transient one-shot systemd-run --on-calendar timer, computed at lockdown-entry time, that fires at exactly lockdown_epoch + MIN_WAKE_AFTER_LOCKDOWN_HOURS and re-runs the (idempotent, self-dedup'd) unlock script -- guaranteeing the alarm gets a correctly-timed chance to fire regardless of what time lockdown actually started.
Added MIN_WAKE_AFTER_LOCKDOWN_HOURS to the enter-script's own config default-fallback block (previously only the unlock script had it).

verification.command: bash -n setup_night_lockdown.sh; shellcheck -s bash setup_night_lockdown.sh (plus extracted enter-script heredoc body)
verification.evidence: Only pre-existing SC1091 info notice; no new warnings.
Transient one-shot timer fired at the exact scheduled second and self-removed via --collect (systemctl status afterward: 'could not be found').

verification.command: sudo setup_night_lockdown.sh setup; sudo DRY_RUN=1 night-lockdown-enter.sh
verification.evidence: Redeployed cleanly; DRY_RUN log now includes 'would schedule a one-shot wake-floor trigger ~8h from now'; real STATE_FILE untouched (still UNLOCKED).

verification.command: Isolated arithmetic check with a scratch STATE_DIR, simulating a 23:30 lockdown
verification.evidence: floor computed as 2026-07-19 07:30:00 -- correctly past the last static 07:00 trigger, demonstrating why the dynamic one-shot is necessary and that the math is correct.

risks: Real end-to-end behavior (does the scheduled one-shot actually re-run the unlock script and start the alarm at the exact floor moment during a genuine overnight cycle) is not yet confirmed live -- deferred to the next real lockdown cycle.

systemd-run --unit name is static (night-lockdown-wake-floor); a stale leftover transient unit with the same name from a crashed prior run could in theory cause this scheduling call to fail. It runs through the existing run() wrapper, which logs a WARN and continues rather than aborting lockdown, and the static triggers remain a fallback for shutdown hours they do cover.
rollback: git revert this commit and re-run 'sudo setup_night_lockdown.sh setup' to redeploy the prior script version.
Manually remove any lingering night-lockdown-wake-floor.timer/.service with 'systemctl stop/reset-failed' if one is ever left active after a revert.
